criteria.description: Documentation of conservative treatment (physical therapy) for minimum 6 weeks
Trial and failure of NSAIDs or other analgesic medication documented
Neurological symptoms present: radiculopathy, myelopathy, or progressive neurological deficit
No prior MRI of same region within 6 months without new clinical findings
Office visit notes within 30 days documenting current symptoms and physical exam

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Low Back Pain
AAN Practice Guidelines for Lumbar Imaging
tips: Include specific PT dates/duration, exercises attempted, and functional limitations. Document red flag symptoms if present. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Documentation of conservative treatment for minimum 4-6 weeks
Failed trial of anti-inflammatory or muscle relaxant medication
Cervical radiculopathy, myelopathy, or neurological deficit on exam
No prior cervical MRI within 6 months unless new neurological findings
Recent office visit notes documenting cervical symptoms and neurological exam

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Neck Pain
NASS Clinical Guidelines for Cervical Radiculopathy
tips: Document specific dermatome distribution of symptoms. Note any upper extremity weakness or reflex changes. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

description: MRI Brain with and without contrast

criteria.description: New or worsening neurological symptoms: headache with red flags, seizures, focal deficit, or altered mental status
For chronic headache: failure of at least 2 prophylactic medications
No prior brain MRI within 12 months unless new neurological findings
Neurological examination documented within 30 days
Clinical indication specified: tumor evaluation, MS workup, seizure workup, stroke evaluation, or persistent headache with red flags

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Headache
AAN Practice Guidelines for Neuroimaging
common_denial_reasons: Routine headache without red flag symptoms
tips: Clearly document red flag symptoms (thunderclap onset, papilledema, focal deficits). For MS workup, note specific neurological findings. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment for minimum 3 months including PT, weight management, and activity modification
Trial and failure of at least 2 classes of medications (NSAIDs, acetaminophen, intra-articular injections)
Radiographic evidence of advanced knee osteoarthritis (Kellgren-Lawrence grade 3-4)
Documented functional impairment with validated outcome measure (WOMAC, Oxford Knee Score, or KOOS)
BMI documented; if BMI >40, documentation of weight management efforts
Orthopedic evaluation within 60 days with surgical plan

clinical_guidelines: AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline for Knee OA
ACR/AF Guidelines for OA Management
common_denial_reasons: No radiographic evidence of advanced OA
tips: Include weight-bearing X-rays showing joint space narrowing. Document specific functional limitations (stairs, walking distance, ADLs). UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment for minimum 3 months including PT and activity modification
Trial of analgesics and/or intra-articular corticosteroid injection
Radiographic evidence of advanced hip joint disease (bone-on-bone, AVN, or dysplasia)
Documented functional impairment (Harris Hip Score or HOOS)
Orthopedic surgical consultation within 60 days
Comorbidity assessment documented (cardiac clearance if indicated)

clinical_guidelines: AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline for Hip OA
ACR/AF Guidelines for OA Management
common_denial_reasons: Imaging does not show advanced disease
tips: AP pelvis and lateral hip X-rays required. Document inability to perform ADLs. Note any avascular necrosis or fracture history. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Documented lumbar radiculopathy correlating with imaging findings
Failed conservative treatment (PT, medications) for minimum 4 weeks
MRI or CT showing disc herniation, stenosis, or nerve root compression
No more than 3 epidural injections in prior 12 months
Failed oral analgesic therapy

clinical_guidelines: ASIPP Guidelines for Interventional Pain Management
NASS Coverage Policy for Lumbar ESI
tips: Document specific nerve root level and corresponding imaging findings. Track injection dates carefully to stay within frequency limits. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Facet-mediated pain pattern documented (axial pain worse with extension, no radiculopathy)
Positive response to diagnostic medial branch block (>50% pain relief)
Failed conservative treatment including PT and medications for 4+ weeks
No more than 4 facet joint injections per spinal region in 12 months

clinical_guidelines: ASIPP Guidelines for Facet Joint Interventions
SIS Practice Guidelines
tips: Always perform and document diagnostic medial branch block results before therapeutic injection. Use dual comparative blocks for best approval rates. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: DSM-5 diagnosis documented with current symptoms warranting 60-minute sessions
Treatment plan with measurable goals and expected duration
GAD-7, PHQ-9, or other validated symptom measure at baseline
Medical necessity for 60-minute session vs 45-minute (90834) documented

clinical_guidelines: APA Practice Guidelines for Mental Health Disorders
AACAP Guidelines for Youth Mental Health
common_denial_reasons: No treatment plan with measurable goals
Medical necessity for 60-min vs 45-min not justified
tips: Document why 60-minute sessions are medically necessary vs 45-minute. Include PHQ-9/GAD-7 scores at each review. Show progress toward goals. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: BMI ≥30 (obesity) or BMI ≥27 with weight-related comorbidity (T2DM, hypertension, dyslipidemia, OSA)
Failed structured diet and exercise program for minimum 3-6 months
For obesity indication: trial and failure of at least one other weight-loss medication (if available on formulary)
Baseline HbA1c, lipid panel, and blood pressure documented
For diabetes indication: HbA1c ≥7.0% on current therapy or inadequate glycemic control
Endocrinology or obesity medicine consultation (payer-dependent)

clinical_guidelines: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes
Endocrine Society Guidelines for Obesity Pharmacotherapy
ACC/AHA Obesity Management Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: Obesity indication not covered under plan
tips: Many plans exclude obesity-only indication. Link to diabetes or cardiovascular risk. Document diet/exercise program with specific dates and provider supervision. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: GI symptoms warranting investigation: dysphagia, persistent GERD, unexplained weight loss, GI bleeding, or anemia
For GERD: failed trial of PPI therapy for 8+ weeks
GI consultation notes with clinical indication
Prior non-invasive workup results (if applicable)

clinical_guidelines: ACG Clinical Guidelines for GERD
ASGE Standards of Practice for EGD Indications
common_denial_reasons: No trial of PPI for GERD symptoms
tips: Document alarm symptoms (dysphagia, weight loss, bleeding) prominently. For Barrett surveillance, include prior pathology reports. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Cardiac symptoms or signs: murmur, dyspnea, chest pain, syncope, or heart failure symptoms
Clinical indication documented: valvular assessment, LV function, pericardial disease, or cardiomyopathy evaluation
No prior echocardiogram within 12 months unless change in clinical status

clinical_guidelines: ACC/AHA Appropriate Use Criteria for Echocardiography
ASE Guidelines for Echocardiography
common_denial_reasons: Recent prior echo without clinical change
tips: Reference ACC Appropriate Use Criteria in your request. Document specific clinical change since last echo if repeat study. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Clinical symptoms of peripheral neuropathy: numbness, tingling, weakness, or pain in extremities
Neurological examination documenting sensory or motor deficits
EMG/NCS being performed to localize lesion, determine severity, or guide treatment
Ordered by or performed by neurologist or physiatrist

clinical_guidelines: AAN Practice Guidelines for EMG/NCS
AANEM Guidelines for Electrodiagnostic Studies
common_denial_reasons: Study not ordered by appropriate specialist
tips: Document specific neurological findings on exam (decreased sensation, weakness grading, reflex changes). Note distribution pattern. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment for minimum 6 weeks including PT, medications, and possible epidural injection
MRI showing disc herniation with nerve root compression correlating with clinical findings
Persistent radiculopathy with positive straight leg raise or neurological deficit
Neurosurgical or orthopedic spine consultation with surgical plan
Documented functional limitation affecting ADLs or work capacity

clinical_guidelines: NASS Guidelines for Lumbar Disc Herniation
AANS/CNS Guidelines for Lumbar Disc Surgery
common_denial_reasons: MRI findings do not correlate with symptoms
tips: Correlate MRI level with clinical dermatome. Document failed epidural injection if performed. Include specific functional limitations. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed comprehensive conservative treatment for minimum 6 months
Imaging confirming instability, spondylolisthesis, stenosis, or degenerative disc disease at surgical level
Persistent symptoms correlating with imaging: back pain, radiculopathy, or neurogenic claudication
Failed less invasive surgical options or clear indication that fusion is primary appropriate intervention
Spine surgeon evaluation with detailed surgical plan including levels and approach
Psychological screening for chronic pain (recommended for fusion candidates)
Tobacco use assessment; smoking cessation if applicable

clinical_guidelines: NASS Guidelines for Lumbar Fusion
AANS/CNS Guidelines for Degenerative Lumbar Disease
common_denial_reasons: Imaging does not support fusion indication
tips: Document specific instability criteria (flexion/extension films). Include psychological evaluation. Address tobacco cessation. Detail all failed treatments with dates. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment and at least one surgical intervention, or not a surgical candidate
Psychological evaluation confirming appropriate candidate without untreated psychiatric conditions
Documented chronic pain condition (CRPS, failed back surgery syndrome, neuropathy) for >6 months
Successful SCS trial with ≥50% pain reduction documented over 5-7 day trial period
Failed adequate trials of multiple pain medication classes
Pain management specialist evaluation and recommendation

clinical_guidelines: NACC Guidelines for SCS
ASIPP Guidelines for Neurostimulation
Neuromodulation Society Best Practices
tips: Psychological eval is REQUIRED by all payers. Document exact trial results with pain scores pre/post. Include all failed medications and surgeries with dates. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Clinical suspicion of obstructive sleep apnea: excessive daytime sleepiness, witnessed apneas, loud snoring
Epworth Sleepiness Scale (ESS) score documented
Risk factors documented: BMI, neck circumference, comorbidities (HTN, CHF, arrhythmia)
Sleep medicine consultation or primary care evaluation for sleep complaints

clinical_guidelines: AASM Clinical Practice Guidelines for OSA Diagnosis
ACP Clinical Guidelines for Sleep Apnea
common_denial_reasons: Prior sleep study within 2 years without clinical change
tips: Include ESS score and STOP-BANG score. Document BMI and neck circumference. Note any cardiac or pulmonary comorbidities. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Cardiac symptoms: chest pain, dyspnea on exertion, or risk factors for CAD
Pre-test probability assessment for CAD (Duke Clinical Score or similar)
Inability to exercise adequately (for pharmacologic stress) or standard exercise stress test indicated
No prior nuclear stress test within 2 years unless change in symptoms

clinical_guidelines: ACC/AHA Appropriate Use Criteria for Nuclear Cardiology
ASNC Imaging Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: Exercise treadmill test not attempted first
Recent prior nuclear study without symptom change
tips: Document pre-test probability. Explain why exercise-only stress test is insufficient. For pharmacologic stress, document inability to exercise. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Signs or symptoms of venous disease: leg swelling, pain, suspected DVT, varicose veins, or venous ulcer
Clinical examination findings documented

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Lower Extremity Venous Ultrasound
SVS Clinical Practice Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: No documented symptoms of venous disease
tips: Document specific symptoms and examination findings. Note any risk factors for DVT (recent surgery, immobilization, malignancy). UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Age-appropriate screening per USPSTF or ACR guidelines (generally age 40+ or 50+ depending on payer)
Risk factor assessment if under standard screening age

clinical_guidelines: USPSTF Breast Cancer Screening Recommendations
ACR Mammography Screening Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: Patient below age threshold without high-risk documentation
tips: Screening mammography typically does not require PA. If PA required for high-risk screening, document family history and risk assessment. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment for minimum 4-6 weeks: PT, NSAIDs, activity modification
MRI showing meniscal tear correlating with clinical findings
Mechanical symptoms: locking, catching, giving way, or persistent effusion
Orthopedic evaluation with surgical recommendation
Age and OA status considered (for patients >50 with OA, evidence less favorable for arthroscopy)

clinical_guidelines: AAOS Appropriate Use Criteria for Knee Arthroscopy
ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Knee Pain
common_denial_reasons: Degenerative meniscal tear in setting of OA (age >50)
tips: Document mechanical symptoms specifically (locking/catching). For patients over 50, emphasize traumatic tear mechanism vs degenerative. Include MRI findings. UHC uses Optum clinical guidelines. Reference their specific policy numbers when available.

criteria.description: Documentation of conservative treatment (physical therapy) for minimum 6 weeks
Trial and failure of NSAIDs or other analgesic medication documented
Neurological symptoms present: radiculopathy, myelopathy, or progressive neurological deficit
No prior MRI of same region within 6 months without new clinical findings
Office visit notes within 30 days documenting current symptoms and physical exam

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Low Back Pain
AAN Practice Guidelines for Lumbar Imaging
tips: Include specific PT dates/duration, exercises attempted, and functional limitations. Document red flag symptoms if present. Aetna uses their Clinical Policy Bulletins (CPBs). Reference the specific CPB number for faster processing.

tips: Document specific instability criteria (flexion/extension films). Include psychological evaluation. Address tobacco cessation. Detail all failed treatments with dates. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment and at least one surgical intervention, or not a surgical candidate
Psychological evaluation confirming appropriate candidate without untreated psychiatric conditions
Documented chronic pain condition (CRPS, failed back surgery syndrome, neuropathy) for >6 months
Successful SCS trial with ≥50% pain reduction documented over 5-7 day trial period
Failed adequate trials of multiple pain medication classes
Pain management specialist evaluation and recommendation

clinical_guidelines: NACC Guidelines for SCS
ASIPP Guidelines for Neurostimulation
Neuromodulation Society Best Practices
tips: Psychological eval is REQUIRED by all payers. Document exact trial results with pain scores pre/post. Include all failed medications and surgeries with dates. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Clinical suspicion of obstructive sleep apnea: excessive daytime sleepiness, witnessed apneas, loud snoring
Epworth Sleepiness Scale (ESS) score documented
Risk factors documented: BMI, neck circumference, comorbidities (HTN, CHF, arrhythmia)
Sleep medicine consultation or primary care evaluation for sleep complaints

clinical_guidelines: AASM Clinical Practice Guidelines for OSA Diagnosis
ACP Clinical Guidelines for Sleep Apnea
common_denial_reasons: Prior sleep study within 2 years without clinical change
tips: Include ESS score and STOP-BANG score. Document BMI and neck circumference. Note any cardiac or pulmonary comorbidities. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Cardiac symptoms: chest pain, dyspnea on exertion, or risk factors for CAD
Pre-test probability assessment for CAD (Duke Clinical Score or similar)
Inability to exercise adequately (for pharmacologic stress) or standard exercise stress test indicated
No prior nuclear stress test within 2 years unless change in symptoms

clinical_guidelines: ACC/AHA Appropriate Use Criteria for Nuclear Cardiology
ASNC Imaging Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: Exercise treadmill test not attempted first
Recent prior nuclear study without symptom change
tips: Document pre-test probability. Explain why exercise-only stress test is insufficient. For pharmacologic stress, document inability to exercise. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Signs or symptoms of venous disease: leg swelling, pain, suspected DVT, varicose veins, or venous ulcer
Clinical examination findings documented

clinical_guidelines: ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Lower Extremity Venous Ultrasound
SVS Clinical Practice Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: No documented symptoms of venous disease
tips: Document specific symptoms and examination findings. Note any risk factors for DVT (recent surgery, immobilization, malignancy). BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Age-appropriate screening per USPSTF or ACR guidelines (generally age 40+ or 50+ depending on payer)
Risk factor assessment if under standard screening age

clinical_guidelines: USPSTF Breast Cancer Screening Recommendations
ACR Mammography Screening Guidelines
common_denial_reasons: Patient below age threshold without high-risk documentation
tips: Screening mammography typically does not require PA. If PA required for high-risk screening, document family history and risk assessment. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment for minimum 4-6 weeks: PT, NSAIDs, activity modification
MRI showing meniscal tear correlating with clinical findings
Mechanical symptoms: locking, catching, giving way, or persistent effusion
Orthopedic evaluation with surgical recommendation
Age and OA status considered (for patients >50 with OA, evidence less favorable for arthroscopy)

clinical_guidelines: AAOS Appropriate Use Criteria for Knee Arthroscopy
ACR Appropriateness Criteria for Knee Pain
common_denial_reasons: Degenerative meniscal tear in setting of OA (age >50)
tips: Document mechanical symptoms specifically (locking/catching). For patients over 50, emphasize traumatic tear mechanism vs degenerative. Include MRI findings. BCBS policies vary by state plan. Verify the specific Blue plan requirements as criteria may differ regionally.
